applied_optimizations.name: Hybrid approach: rocBLAS GEMM + PyTorch GroupNorm + Triton fused elementwise
applied_optimizations.description: Key insight: rocBLAS GEMM and PyTorch GroupNorm are already highly optimized. Use them directly. Only use Triton for fusing LeakyReLU + element-wise double (x+x=2*x) into a single in-place kernel. This minimizes kernel launch overhead while leveraging the best available implementation for each operation. BLOCK_SIZE=1024 provides good utilization on MI355X.

attempted_but_failed.name: Full Triton matmul kernel
attempted_but_failed.reason: Hand-written Triton matmul cannot beat highly-tuned rocBLAS
attempted_but_failed.error: Initial implementation was 2.93ms (2.7x slower than reference)

attempted_but_failed.name: Optimized Triton matmul with larger blocks
attempted_but_failed.reason: Shared memory limits and correctness issues with different block sizes
attempted_but_failed.error: Best Triton-only matmul achieved 1.86ms, still 1.7x slower than rocBLAS

attempted_but_failed.name: Fused Triton GroupNorm kernel (per batch-group)
attempted_but_failed.reason: Too many small kernel launches (batch×groups=524288) with high overhead

attempted_but_failed.name: Row-wise fused GroupNorm kernel (one block per row)
attempted_but_failed.reason: Sequential group processing within block introduces loop overhead

attempted_but_failed.name: 2D grid fused GroupNorm kernel
attempted_but_failed.reason: Still has high launch overhead, memory bandwidth bound

attempted_but_failed.name: torch.compile with Triton kernels
attempted_but_failed.reason: Incompatibility between torch.compile and Triton on ROCm

notes: Key learning: Don't try to beat rocBLAS with hand-written Triton matmul - it's already highly optimized
PyTorch GroupNorm is also extremely well optimized - fusing it in Triton doesn't help for small channels_per_group
Hybrid approach works best: use rocBLAS for GEMM, PyTorch for GroupNorm, Triton only for fusing simple elementwise ops
The speedup is modest (1.01x) because the baseline is already well-optimized
Main optimization was reducing kernel launch overhead by fusing LeakyReLU + Double into one in-place Triton kernel
BLOCK_SIZE=1024 works well for elementwise kernel on MI355X (good wavefront utilization)
For problems with more elementwise operations relative to GEMM, Triton fusion would provide more benefit

attempted_but_failed.reason: After 4 consecutive trials with no output (trials 2-5), the agent is clearly unable to make further progress. The best score of 60.10 was achieved in trial 1 with a hybrid approach (rocBLAS GEMM + PyT

global_notes: For AMD MI355X, the optimal strategy is to leverage rocBLAS for GEMM operations
PyTorch's GroupNorm is highly optimized - don't try to beat it with Triton for small channel groups
Triton is best used for fusing elementwise operations that would otherwise require multiple kernel launches
torch.compile integration with Triton has issues on ROCm - avoid combining them
The problem structure (86% GEMM time) limits optimization potential - most time is in already-optimized rocBLAS
